title: VPC Networking Fundamentals + VPC Peering
aim: To create a VPC network with firewall rules, deploy VMs in different zones, test connectivity, and set up VPC Network Peering between two VPC networks.

steps.title: Part A: VPC Networking Fundamentals
steps.description: Create a VPC network and test connectivity between VMs.

steps.title: Create a VPC Network
steps.description: Go to VPC network > Create VPC Network. Name: mynetwork. Subnet mode: Automatic. Check all 4 default firewall rules. Click Create.

steps.title: Create VM Instances
steps.description: Create two VMs (mynet-us-vm and mynet-eu-vm) in different regions using the 'mynetwork' VPC.

steps.title: Test Connectivity
steps.description: SSH into one VM and ping both the internal and external IPs of the other VM.

steps.title: Part B: VPC Network Peering
steps.description: Connect two separate VPC networks privately.

steps.title: Set Up Peering
steps.description: Create two VPCs (network-a and network-b). Create a peering connection from A to B, and another from B to A. Verify the status is 'Active'.

commands.purpose: Create a VM in a specific VPC network

commands.cmd: gcloud compute networks peerings create peer-ab --network=network-a --peer-network=network-b --auto-create-routes

viva.question: What is a VPC?
viva.answer: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a private network environment on Google Cloud that provides networking for your cloud resources.

viva.question: What is VPC Network Peering?
viva.answer: It allows two VPC networks to connect internally over the Google Cloud network, making them behave as if they were part of the same network.

viva.question: Is VPC Peering transitive?
viva.answer: No, peering is non-transitive. If A is peered with B and B is peered with C, A and C are NOT peered unless you create a direct connection between them.

notes: VPC Peering works even across different Google Cloud projects or organizations.
